    35  var errInvalidToken = errors.New("invalid bearer token")
    36
    37  // ProtocolAuthenticator allows a websocket connection to provide a bearer token as a subprotocol
    38  // in the format "base64url.bearer.authorization.<base64url-without-padding(bearer-token)>"
    39  type ProtocolAuthenticator struct {
    40          // auth is the token authenticator to use to validate the token
    41          auth authenticator.Token
    42  }
    43
    44  func NewProtocolAuthenticator(auth authenticator.Token) *ProtocolAuthenticator {
    45          return &ProtocolAuthenticator{auth}
    46  }
    47
    48  func (a *ProtocolAuthenticator) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {
    49          // Only accept websocket connections
    50          if !wsstream.IsWebSocketRequest(req) {
    51                  return nil, false, nil
    52          }
    53
    54          token := ""
    55          sawTokenProtocol := false
    56          filteredProtocols := []string{}
    57          for _, protocolHeader := range req.Header[protocolHeader] {
    58                  for _, protocol := range strings.Split(protocolHeader, ",") {
    59                          protocol = strings.TrimSpace(protocol)
    60
    61                          if !strings.HasPrefix(protocol, bearerProtocolPrefix) {
    62                                  filteredProtocols = append(filteredProtocols, protocol)
    63                                  continue
    64                          }
    65
    66                          if sawTokenProtocol {
    67                                  return nil, false, errors.New("multiple base64.bearer.authorization tokens specified")
    68                          }
    69                          sawTokenProtocol = true
    70
    71                          encodedToken := strings.TrimPrefix(protocol, bearerProtocolPrefix)
    72                          decodedToken, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(encodedToken)
    73                          if err != nil {
    74                                  return nil, false, errors.New("invalid base64.bearer.authorization token encoding")
    75                          }
    76                          if !utf8.Valid(decodedToken) {
    77                                  return nil, false, errors.New("invalid base64.bearer.authorization token")
    78                          }
    79                          token = string(decodedToken)
    80                  }
    81          }
    82
    83          // Must pass at least one other subprotocol so that we can remove the one containing the bearer token,
    84          // and there is at least one to echo back to the client
    85          if len(token) > 0 && len(filteredProtocols) == 0 {
    86                  return nil, false, errors.New("missing additional subprotocol")
    87          }
    88
    89          if len(token) == 0 {
    90                  return nil, false, nil
    91          }
    92
    93          resp, ok, err := a.auth.AuthenticateToken(req.Context(), token)
    94
    95          // on success, remove the protocol with the token
    96          if ok {
    97                  // https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6455#section-11.3.4 indicates the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header may appear multiple times
    98                  // in a request, and is logically the same as a single Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header field that contains all values
    99                  req.Header.Set(protocolHeader, strings.Join(filteredProtocols, ","))
   100          }
   101
   102          // If the token authenticator didn't error, provide a default error
   103          if !ok && err == nil {
   104                  err = errInvalidToken
   105          }
   106
   107          return resp, ok, err
   108  }

    44
    45          // TokenAccessReviewClient is a client to do token review. It can be nil. Then every token is ignored.
    46          TokenAccessReviewClient authenticationclient.AuthenticationV1Interface
    47
    48          // TokenAccessReviewTimeout specifies a time limit for requests made by the authorization webhook client.
    49          TokenAccessReviewTimeout time.Duration
    50
    51          // WebhookRetryBackoff specifies the backoff parameters for the authentication webhook retry logic.
    52          // This allows us to configure the sleep time at each iteration and the maximum number of retries allowed

    69  func (c DelegatingAuthenticatorConfig) New() (authenticator.Request, *spec.SecurityDefinitions, error) {
    70          authenticators := []authenticator.Request{}
    71          securityDefinitions := spec.SecurityDefinitions{}

    76                  requestHeaderAuthenticator := headerrequest.NewDynamicVerifyOptionsSecure(
    77                          c.RequestHeaderConfig.CAContentProvider.VerifyOptions,
    78                          c.RequestHeaderConfig.AllowedClientNames,
    79                          c.RequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
    80                          c.RequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
    81                          c.RequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
    82                          c.RequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
    83                  )
    84                  authenticators = append(authenticators, requestHeaderAuthenticator)
    85          }
    86
    87          // x509 client cert auth
    88          if c.ClientCertificateCAContentProvider != nil {
    89                  authenticators = append(authenticators, x509.NewDynamic(c.ClientCertificateCAContentProvider.VerifyOptions, x509.CommonNameUserConversion))
    90          }

    93                  if c.WebhookRetryBackoff == nil {

    97                          RecordRequestTotal:   RecordRequestTotal,
    98                          RecordRequestLatency: RecordRequestLatency,
    99                  })

   101                          return nil, nil, err
   102                  }
   103                  cachingTokenAuth := cache.New(tokenAuth, false, c.CacheTTL, c.CacheTTL)
   104                  authenticators = append(authenticators, bearertoken.New(cachingTokenAuth), websocket.NewProtocolAuthenticator(cachingTokenAuth))
   105
   106                  securityDefinitions["BearerToken"] = &spec.SecurityScheme{
   107                          SecuritySchemeProps: spec.SecuritySchemeProps{
   108                                  Type:        "apiKey",
   109                                  Name:        "authorization",
   110                                  In:          "header",
   111                                  Description: "Bearer Token authentication",
   112                          },
   113                  }
   114          }
   115
   116          if len(authenticators) == 0 {
   117                  if c.Anonymous != nil && c.Anonymous.Enabled {
   118                          return anonymous.NewAuthenticator(c.Anonymous.Conditions), &securityDefinitions, nil
   119                  }
   120                  return nil, nil, errors.New("no authentication method configured")
   121          }

   125                  authenticator = unionauth.NewFailOnError(authenticator, anonymous.NewAuthenticator(c.Anonymous.Conditions))
   126          }
   127          return authenticator, &securityDefinitions, nil
   128  }

    42  // WithAuthentication creates an http handler that tries to authenticate the given request as a user, and then
    43  // stores any such user found onto the provided context for the request. If authentication fails or returns an error
    44  // the failed handler is used. On success, "Authorization" header is removed from the request and handler
    45  // is invoked to serve the request.
    46  func WithAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig) http.Handler {
    47          return withAuthentication(handler, auth, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, recordAuthenticationMetrics)
    48  }

    50  func withAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig, metrics authenticationRecordMetricsFunc) http.Handler {
    51          if auth == nil {
    52                  klog.Warning("Authentication is disabled")
    53                  return handler
    54          }
    55          standardRequestHeaderConfig := &authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig{
    56                  UsernameHeaders:     headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-User"},
    57                  UIDHeaders:          headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Uid"},
    58                  GroupHeaders:        headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Group"},
    59                  ExtraHeaderPrefixes: headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Extra-"},
    60          }
    61          return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    62                  authenticationStart := time.Now()
    63
    64                  if len(apiAuds) > 0 {
    65                          req = req.WithContext(authenticator.WithAudiences(req.Context(), apiAuds))
    66                  }
    67                  resp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)
    68                  authenticationFinish := time.Now()
    69                  defer func() {
    70                          metrics(req.Context(), resp, ok, err, apiAuds, authenticationStart, authenticationFinish)
    71                  }()
    72                  if err != nil || !ok {
    73                          if err != nil {
    74                                  klog.ErrorS(err, "Unable to authenticate the request")
    75                          }
    76                          failed.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    77                          return
    78                  }
    79
    80                  if !audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, resp.Audiences) {
    81                          err = fmt.Errorf("unable to match the audience: %v , accepted: %v", resp.Audiences, apiAuds)
    82                          klog.Error(err)
    83                          failed.ServeHTTP(w, req)
    84                          return
    85                  }

    87                  // authorization header is not required anymore in case of a successful authentication.
    88                  req.Header.Del("Authorization")

    90                  // delete standard front proxy headers
    91                  headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(
    92                          req.Header,
    93                          standardRequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
    94                          standardRequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
    95                          standardRequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
    96                          standardRequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
    97                  )
    98
    99                  // also delete any custom front proxy headers
   100                  if requestHeaderConfig != nil {
   101                          headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(
   102                                  req.Header,
   103                                  requestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
   104                                  requestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
   105                                  requestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
   106                                  requestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
   107                          )
   108                  }

   110                  // http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,
   111                  // see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.
   112                  // Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these
   113                  // connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the
   114                  // performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).

   117                          // and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection
   118                          // https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c
   119                          w.Header().Set("Connection", "close")
   120                  }
   121
   122                  req = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))
   123                  handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
   124          })
   125  }

   127  func Unauthorized(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {
   128          return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
   129                  // http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,
   130                  // see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.
   131                  // Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these
   132                  // connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the
   133                  // performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).
   134                  if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 {
   135                          // limit this connection to just this request,
   136                          // and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection
   137                          // https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c
   138                          w.Header().Set("Connection", "close")
   139                  }
   140                  ctx := req.Context()
   141                  requestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)
   142                  if !found {
   143                          responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New("no RequestInfo found in the context"))
   144                          return
   145                  }
   146
   147                  gv := schema.GroupVersion{Group: requestInfo.APIGroup, Version: requestInfo.APIVersion}
   148                  responsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewUnauthorized("Unauthorized"), s, gv, w, req)
   149          })
   150  }

   152  func audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, responseAudiences authenticator.Audiences) bool {
   153          if len(apiAuds) == 0 || len(responseAudiences) == 0 {
   154                  return true
   155          }
   156
   157          return len(apiAuds.Intersect(responseAudiences)) > 0
   158  }

   161          if u.GetName() == user.Anonymous {
   162                  return true

   164          for _, group := range u.GetGroups() {
   165                  if group == user.AllUnauthenticated {
   166                          return true
   167                  }
   168          }
   169          return false
   170  }

## Structure of `/workspace/answer.json`

The file is organized into **7 categories** covering **33 Go source files** across the kubernetes/kubernetes v1.32.0 tree, plus a 16-step end-to-end authentication flow summary.

### Category Breakdown

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `pkg/kubeapiserver/authenticator/config.go` | **Primary chain builder.** `Config.New()` assembles all enabled authenticators into the ordered union pipeline. Contains `newLegacyServiceAccountAuthenticator`, `newServiceAccountAuthenticator`, `newWebhookTokenAuthenticator`, `newJWTAuthenticator`, `newAuthenticatorFromTokenFile`, and the hot-reload `authenticationConfigUpdater`. |
| `pkg/kubeapiserver/options/authentication.go` | CLI/config-file option structs (`BuiltInAuthenticationOptions` and per-method sub-structs). `ToAuthenticationConfig()` converts flags → `Config`; `ApplyTo()` wires the chain into the generic server. |

| `staging/.../authenticatorfactory/loopback.go` | `NewFromTokens()` — loopback authenticator for in-process clients. |
